run_notes: WIN-015 Cycle 2 new holes (publication audit + absence-of-evidence). WIN-004/005 Cycle 2: wrong Terra-Nova EPSL DOI in WIN-005.

patches.rationale: Wrong DOI: j.epsl.2017.06.036 resolves to Fukai & Yokoyama (2017) on Neodymium isotope heterogeneity in chondrites — a completely unrelated geochemistry paper. The correct Terra-Nova et al. (2017) 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches' paper is in Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors, DOI j.pepi.2017.03.002. Identified in WIN-004 Cycle 2 review; bug is in WIN-005 (WIN-004 already has correct PEPI DOI).

patches.find: Published only in non-peer-reviewed conference proceedings.
patches.replace: Published primarily in non-peer-reviewed conference proceedings, with no peer-reviewed replication of the Faraday cage penetration claim identified in standard scientific databases.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon Cycle 2 hole 5: the absolute 'only in non-peer-reviewed' claim could be undermined if a dome defender finds a counterexample (even a minor journal). Softening to 'primarily' and 'no peer-reviewed replication identified' is accurate, defensible, and harder to knock down with a single citation.

patches.find: Thousands of precision experiments globally rely on standard Faraday shielding with no anomalous penetration observed.
patches.replace: Thousands of precision experiments globally rely on standard Faraday shielding with no anomalous penetration observed — indirect but strong evidence against any scalar wave effect at all energy scales.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon Cycle 2 hole 6: the absence-of-evidence argument is real but should be acknowledged as indirect. Adding the qualification 'indirect but strong' preempts the dome defender's 'those experiments don't test Meyl's specific frequency range' objection while preserving the force of the argument.
